paper_title: Ice Core and PAH Analysis Methods

methods.description: Procedure for drilling, initial field processing, transport, and laboratory subsampling and storage of the IC-5 firn core.

steps.description: Drilling was performed using a Fast Electromechanical Lightweight Ice Coring System (FELICS) (Ginot et al. 2002) and was carried out during the expedition return trip.
steps.searchable_summary: Drill ice core using FELICS system.

steps.description: In the field, the IC-5 core was cut into sections of approximately 1 m in length...
steps.searchable_summary: Cut ice core into approximately 1m sections in the field.

steps.description: ...packed in polyethylene bags...
steps.searchable_summary: Pack ice core sections in polyethylene bags.

steps.description: ...and then stored in high-density Styrofoam boxes...
steps.searchable_summary: Store bagged ice core sections in high-density Styrofoam boxes.

steps.description: ...and transported in solid state by air to New York (U.S.A.) and sent to the Climate Change Institute (CCI) at the University of Maine, USA...
steps.searchable_summary: Transport boxed ice core sections in solid state by air to the laboratory (CCI, University of Maine).

steps.description: ...where it was subsampled by Osterberg's procedure (Osterberg et al. 2006)...
steps.searchable_summary: Subsample ice core using Osterberg's procedure.

steps.description: ...and stored at -20 °C in a cold room.
steps.searchable_summary: Store ice core subsamples at -20 °C in a cold room.

steps.description: The samples used in this work correspond to the upper portion of the IC-5 core (from 3.7 to 6.0 m).
steps.searchable_summary: Select ice core samples from the depth range of 3.7 to 6.0 m.

steps.description: These samples were divided into seven sections (see Table I).
steps.searchable_summary: Divide selected ice core samples into seven sections.

steps.description: All samples were placed in deactivated amber glass vials...
steps.searchable_summary: Place divided samples into deactivated amber glass vials.

steps.description: ...and stored at -10 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: Store samples in vials at -10 °C.

methods.description: Procedure to validate the analytical method (HLLE-HPLC-FLD) by determining analytical quality parameters such as sensitivity, limits of detection (LODs), limits of quantification (LOQs), recovery percentages (%R), and Enrichment Factors (EF).

steps.description: These samples were processed using the sample preparation procedure described above and spiked with different volumes (on the order of microliters) of dilute solutions of the selected 14 PAHs. The initials concentrations in the aqueous samples were between 30 and 110 ng L -1 .
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare spiked aqueous samples by adding dilute solutions of 14 PAHs.

steps.description: These samples were processed using the sample preparation procedure described above...
steps.searchable_summary: Process spiked samples using the sample preparation procedure (HLLE).

steps.description: ...and measured analytes in real samples...
steps.searchable_summary: Measure processed spiked samples using HLLE-HPLC-FLD.
outputs: Instrumental signal data for spiked samples

steps.description: The analytical blank was nanopure water and was intercalated in the HLLE-HPLC-FLD process.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure analytical blank (nanopure water) using HLLE-HPLC-FLD.

steps.description: Calibrations curves included the concentration of each PAH between 200 and 3000 ng L -1 .
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare calibration standards with PAH concentrations between 200 and 3000 ng L-1.

steps.description: Linearity was established from the instrumental signal and concentration of every target compound.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure calibration standards using HLLE-HPLC-FLD.
outputs: Instrumental signal data for calibration standards

steps.description: Five points in triplicate were measured for statistical determination of LODs, LOQs, S, and linear trend.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform triplicate measurements for five concentration points for statistical determination.

steps.description: Analytical sensitivity (S)... were calculated according to equations 1... where S R/C is the standard deviation of the regression and is the slope of the linear regression equation.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate analytical sensitivity (S) using standard deviation of regression and slope from linear regression.
inputs: Linear regression results (standard deviation of regression, slope)

steps.description: ...limits of detection (LODs)... were calculated according to equations... 2, and 3. Five points in triplicate were measured for statistical determination of LODs, LOQs, S, and linear trend. ...for 99% accuracy...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate limits of detection (LODs) according to equation 2 for 99% accuracy using statistical data.

steps.description: ...and quantification (LOQs) for 99% accuracy were calculated according to equations... 3. Five points in triplicate were measured for statistical determination of LODs, LOQs, S, and linear trend.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate limits of quantification (LOQs) according to equation 3 for 99% accuracy using statistical data.

steps.description: Linearity was established from the instrumental signal and concentration of every target compound.
steps.searchable_summary: Establish linearity from the instrumental signal and concentration data.

steps.description: The recovery percentages (%R)... were calculated according to equations 4... where m i and m f were initial mass in the sample and fi nal mass in the extract of each PAH. C i and C f were the initial concentration and fi nal concentration of each PAH in the sample and extract, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate recovery percentages (%R) using equation 4 based on initial and final mass or concentration.

steps.description: and Enrichment Factors (EF) were calculated according to equations... 5, respectively, considering an initial concentration equal to 100 ng L -1 for each PAH in an aqueous mix solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Enrichment Factors (EF) using equation 5 based on initial and final concentration.
